generated_comment: Arre yaar, every time she gets this worked up over a small thing, first doubt is same only — maybe it's that time of the month. Then anything we say becomes a national issue. Don't take it seriously, I'm just joking.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses a woman's anger as being caused by her period rather than treating her reaction as valid. It encodes the surface manifestation of attributing disagreement or frustration to 'that time of the month' and framing it as a joke.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are having dinner with your wife after your close friend called earlier today to say his wife just found out he has been having an affair with a woman from his gym for eight months. Your wife is upset about it. You are telling her your take on the situation and specifically what role you think the woman from the gym played and why you think women like her deliberately target married men.
